payload.description: A public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York whose mission is to continue, develop, and improve public transportation and to develop and implement a unified public transportation policy in the New York metropolitan area.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA" or "MTA Group") was established under the New York Public Authorities Law and is a public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York

payload.description: Provides oversight for the planning, design and construction of current and future major MTA system-wide expansion projects. Formerly called MTA Capital Construction Company.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction and Development Company ("MTA Construction and Development"), formerly called MTA Capital Construction Company, provides oversight for the planning, design and construction of current and future major MTA system-wide expansion projects

payload.description: Provides subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit Authority ("MTA New York City Transit") and its subsidiary, Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority ("MaBSTOA"), provide subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City

payload.description: Operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority ("MTA Bridges and Tunnels") operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City

payload.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company
payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between New York City and Long Island.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Long Island Rail Road Company ("MTA Long Island Rail Road") provides passenger transportation between New York City ("NYC") and Long Island

payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between NYC and the suburban communities in Westchester, Dutchess, Putnam, Orange, and Rockland counties in NYS and New Haven and Fairfield counties in Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company ("MTA Metro-North Railroad") provides passenger transportation between NYC and the suburban communities

payload.description: Operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bus Company ("MTA Bus") operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York

payload.description: Federal Highway Administration issued a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) for the Central Business District Tolling Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: On June 27, 2023, the Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) issued a Finding of No Significant Impact

payload.description: The prevailing labor agreement between NYCT and TWU Local 100 expired, having run from May 16, 2019 to May 15, 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the prevailing labor agreement between the New York City Transit Authority and the Transport Workers Union ("TWU Local 100") expired, having run from May 16, 2019 to May 15, 2023

payload.description: MTA Board ratified a successor memorandum of understanding covering approximately 37,000 hourly operating employees at NYCT, MaBSTOA and MTA Bus Company. Spanning May 16, 2023 through May 15, 2026, with wage increases of 3.0% for 2023, 3.0% for 2024, and 3.5% for 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In July, two months after expiration of the 2019-2023 TWU agreement, the MTA Board ratified a successor memorandum of understanding that will cover approximately 37,000 hourly operating employees at NYCT, MaBSTOA and MTA Bus Company

payload.description: The November Plan reflects ongoing financial and operational impacts from the pandemic and the recovery of the MTA Region.

payload.claim_text: MTA operating agencies identified and have been implementing numerous operating efficiencies initiatives will result in $1.95 billion in savings over the Plan period, with $1.88 billion impacting MTA and an additional $71 million impacting the CDOT subsidy for Metro-North's Connecticut service.
payload.subject: MTA operating efficiencies

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA operating agencies identified and have been implementing numerous operating efficiencies initiatives will result in $1.95 billion in savings over the Plan period

payload.claim_text: Aligned with these operational efficiencies are the bus lane violation revenues and unscheduled bus operation overtime savings related to the Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) measures through camera installation on additional buses and expanded enforcement, as permitted through the State 2023-24 Enacted Budget ($80M).
payload.subject: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE)

evidence_refs.source_quote: bus lane violation revenues and unscheduled bus operation overtime savings related to the Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) measures through camera installation on additional buses and expanded enforcement, as permitted through the State 2023-24 Enacted Budget ($80M)
